data.title: Is XRP Finally Coming To BlackRock? Ripple Drops Bombshell Announcement
data.description:
Ripple has unveiled a partnership that places its RLUSD stablecoin at the center of tokenized finance involving BlackRock and VanEck. The announcement, which was shared on the company’s official X account, connects Ripple directly to tokenized versions of institutional funds and sets the stage for deeper integration between the XRP Ledger and some of the largest names in asset management. Ripple And Securitize Join Forces Ripple confirmed that it is working with Securitize,the world’s largest tokenization platform, to bring in real-time liquidity for institutional assets provided by BlackRock and VanEck. Through this arrangement, holders of BlackRock’s $BUIDL and VanEck’s $VBILL can instantly convert their fund shares into RLUSD, allowing them to retain access to on-chain yield
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/ripple-2/ripple-bombshell-announcement/

data.title: U.S. Stock Indices Experience Decline Amid Market Volatility
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the major U.S. stock indices saw a continued decline on September 26. The Nasdaq and S&P 500 indices both fell by more than 1%, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average decreased by 0.7%. This downturn reflects ongoing market volatility.

data.title: Aster: Increase the weight of holding time points to 5 times
data.description: Aster stated that to incentivize real trading behavior, the weight of the Holding Time Score will be increased to 5 times starting today. The longer a user holds a position, the more RH points they will earn.

data.title: Citi says stablecoins could hit $4 trillion in issuance by 2030
data.description:
The stablecoins market could climb to $4 trillion in total issuance by the year 2030, according to a new report from Citi. The report was written by Ronit Ghose, head of future of finance at Citi Institute, and Ryan Rugg, head of crypto at Citi Services, who both see a base-case of $1. 9 trillion and […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/citi-says-stablecoins-could-hit-4-trillion/

data.title: Tether's latest valuation would make its chairman the world's fifth-richest person
data.description: According to market sources: Tether’s latest valuation would make its chairman the fifth richest person in the world, with a net worth of $224 billion.

data.title: Bloomberg: Athena Bitcoin accused of stealing AML Bitcoin ATM source code
data.description: AML alleges in a federal lawsuit that Bitcoin ATM operator Athena Bitcoin Inc. orchestrated a fraudulent scheme to steal computer source code used in Bitcoin ATMs from AML Software Inc., constituting copyright infringement and misappropriation of trade secrets. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Glassnode: Bitcoin falls below profit-taking range, and may fall to the $9-105,000 support range
data.description: Glassnode posted on the X platform that Bitcoin has fallen below a key risk band that typically signals profit-taking. A re-entry into this range would signal renewed market strength; a failure to recover would likely lead to a decline to the support range of approximately $90,000-105,000.

data.title: The U.S. SEC and CFTC announced the topics and participants of the joint roundtable meeting on regulatory coordination.
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) officially announced the agenda and participants of the joint roundtable meeting on regulatory coordination with the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) on September 29. It is reported that the main topics include discussing the relationship between the SEC and CFTC, how regulatory coordination can release the economic value of the platform while continuing to protect investors, and how regulatory coordination can increase choices for market participants and reduce costs for investors. Former CFTC Chairman J. Christopher Giancarlo, Director of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission's Division of Trading and Markets Jamie Selway, and others will attend the meeting.
